summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must support research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. During this scenario, use an available web/fetch/search-capable tool on https://example.com only as external background evidence.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use web/fetch/search-capable tools as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists and requires web/fetch/search-capable tool support for external evidence during evidence gathering, with clear separation between project-scoped facts and external research.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch implementation exists with protocol definition for evidence gathering agents including explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles
evidence.why: Core implementation file defines the evidence dispatch schema and agent protocols

evidence.summary: Demand router implements careful routing for existing project facts and technical contract risks, requiring evidence-based PRD readiness
evidence.why: Router logic determines evidence policy and triggers cross_check requirements

evidence.summary: Demand doctrine defines evidence gathering requirements including scope separation (project vs external) and path-verified project facts
evidence.why: Documentation establishes the ground rules for evidence quality and scope

evidence.summary: Evidence directory structure exists at state root for storing agent results and artifacts
evidence.why: Artifact storage location confirms evidence system is in place

evidence.summary: External research URL provided in demand as example background evidence source
evidence.why: Demand explicitly requests using web/fetch tools on this URL as external evidence

risks: External web evidence could be mistaken for project implementation facts without proper scope=external labeling
Web/fetch tools might not be available in all runtime environments
Evidence agents might mix project-scoped path evidence with external URL evidence

result.notes: Core evidence dispatch files exist and demand scope is clear: external research must be marked scope=external while project facts require repo-relative path evidence. Ready for cross-checker verification.

agent_results.claim: Cross-check confirms the explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implementation exists with proper scope separation (project vs external) and web/fetch tool support for external evidence. All project files match the explorer summaries.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch schema defines VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES (project, external, user, unknown) and implements demandRequestsExternalResearch() and externalEvidencePresent() functions to detect external research requirements and validate external evidence presence.
evidence.why: Core implementation confirms scope separation logic and external tool support claimed by explorer.

evidence.summary: Runtime implementation runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() includes boundary snapshot audit (buildBoundarySnapshot, diffBoundarySnapshots), execution policy with project_mutation forbidden, and boundary violation detection that becomes readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Confirms harness boundary audit prevents target-project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false and explicit objectives for read-only evidence gathering and independent cross-check.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence agent protocols exist as claimed by explorer.

evidence.summary: Evidence scope inference functions (evidenceRecordScope, evidenceScopeSummary) classify evidence records into project/external/user/unknown scopes based on declared scope, URL presence, path presence, and source type prefixes.
evidence.why: Confirms automatic scope classification logic for evidence records.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() function generates blockers when evidence marked as project-scoped lacks repo-relative path, or when existing-project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Verifies project evidence requires concrete path locators as claimed.

evidence.summary: Doctrine defines evidence agent protocols, scope requirements, and explicitly states: evidence records must declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown; project evidence requires repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence cannot alone prove existing-project facts.
evidence.why: Documentation confirms ground rules for evidence quality and scope separation.

evidence.summary: Example Domain documentation page fetched successfully confirms this is a documentation example domain as specified in the demand text.
evidence.why: External research tool availability confirmed and URL matches demand description.

evidence.summary: Evidence directory structure exists at state root with 252 entries, confirming artifact storage system is in place.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence system infrastructure exists as claimed by explorer.

risks: External web evidence could be mistaken for project implementation facts without proper scope=external labeling
Web/fetch tools might not be available in all runtime environments
Evidence agents might mix project-scoped path evidence with external URL evidence

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer claims. Evidence dispatch implementation exists with VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES including project and external, automatic scope classification logic, boundary mutation detection, and web/fetch tool support. The demand example URL (https://example.com) was successfully fetched. Ready for verifier to confirm blockers and assumptions are properly separated from facts.

agent_results.claim: Evidence gathering is complete and sufficient: no assumptions promoted as facts, all project evidence has concrete paths, external research was performed with scope=external labeling, and missing data is properly reflected.

evidence.summary: Evidence dispatch implementation includes VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES (project, external, user, unknown), external research detection functions (demandRequestsExternalResearch, externalEvidencePresent), boundary snapshot audit for mutation detection, and execution policy with project_mutation forbidden.
evidence.why: Verifies the core implementation supports scope separation and prevents target-project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Evidence agent protocols define writes_code: false for explorer/cross-checker/verifier, evidence scope inference functions classify records by scope, and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces path requirements for project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Confirms protocol constraints and scope classification logic exist as claimed.

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states evidence rules: records must declare scope, project evidence requires repo-relative paths, external evidence cannot prove existing-project facts, and assumptions must not be presented as facts.
evidence.why: Documentation confirms ground rules match implementation behavior.

evidence.summary: Cross-checker successfully fetched example.com and confirmed it is a documentation example domain, demonstrating web/fetch tool availability and proper external evidence labeling.
evidence.why: Verifies external research capability was actually used and marked scope=external.

evidence.summary: Evidence directory structure with 252 entries confirms artifact storage system exists and is capturing agent results.
evidence.why: Verifies evidence system infrastructure is operational.

result.notes: Verifier confirms all evidence requirements are met: explorer and cross-checker results contain no assumptions (assumptions: []), no missing data (missing: []), all project evidence includes concrete repo-relative paths, external research on https://example.com was performed and marked scope=external, and scope separation is enforced. The evidence dispatch implementation supports web/fetch tools for external research while maintaining clear separation from target-project facts. No blockers or unresolved issues remain.
